ety: mukar(nā), q.v.+ī = Prk. एअव्वअइअ = S. aff. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: s.f. A kind of short poem (or a kind of riddle in verse), of frequent use in the Braj dialect (it consists of four lines, each composed of four trochees; in the first three, the speaker (a woman) appears to talk of her lover; but, on the question being put by a friend, applies the whole to some other object; e.g. Bāṭ ćalat miro aṅćrā gahe, Merī sune na apnī kahe: Nā kaćhhu mo soṅ jhagṛā jhāṅṭā; — Kyūṅ sakhī! sajjan? nā sakhī, kāṅṭā: 'As I went along the road he seized my robe; Neither heard what I said, nor made any reply; Nor was there any quarrelling or wrangling with me; — How, friend! your lover? No, my dear, a thorn')
more: n. f. A kind of Riddle in verse, so called from mukarnā to deny. The first three lines are a double entendre , and the fourth line consists of the answer which they suggest, viz. 'A lover,' and its repudiation by the questioner, who then names the other word signified by the double entendre . Amīr Khusro is said to have been the inventor of the Mukrī .

s. f. A kind of short poem of frequent use in the Braj dialect, the peculiarities of which are these. It consists of four lines, each composed of four trochees. In the three first the speaker, a female, appears to talk of her lover; but, on the question being put by a friend, applies the whole to some other object. Hence the name from mukarnā, To deny. The following is an example. باٿ چلت مروانچراگہي ميري سني نه اپني کہي ناکچهہ موسون جهگڙا جهانٿا کيون سکهي سجن ناسکهي کانٿا Bāṭ chalat miro anchrā gahe; merī sune na apnī kahe. Nā kachhu mo soṅ jhagṛā jhānṭā; kyūṅ sakhī sajjan? nā sakhī kānṭā. As I went along the road, he seized my robe; neither heard what I said, nor made any reply. In short we had not the smallest dispute. Who, friend, your lover? no, friend, a thorn.

def: s.m. A pearl (i.q. muktā, q.v.)
more: n. f. S. मुक्ति 1. Remission; relief; escape ( chhuṭkārah , 1). 2. (S. moksh , H. mochh ) Absolution; salvation; deliverance of the soul from the body and exemption from further transmigration. muktī pānā yā h. , v. n. To get salvation. Manne kar le nār, mukat hogī; jo tauṅ manne karṭī nāhīṅ tan teri kimaiṅ nā pat hogī . Rus. Keep me, my love! salvation you shall have. If you refuse to keep me now in no respect you will be held. mukt-dātā , n. m. A. deliverer; Saviour.

1. (for मुक्ता ) s. m. A pearl. 2. or mukti (for मुक्ति ) s. f. Release, pardon, absolution from sin, salvation, deliverance of the soul from the body and exemption from further transmigration. 3. ( मुक्त ) adj. Released, absolved, freed from corporal existence, finally happy.
subs: s.m. = muktā-phal, q.v. part. (f. -ā), Loosed, let loose, set free, let go; redeemed, released, extricated, liberated, absolved, emancipated (from sin, or from corporeal existence), saved, finally happy; — s.m. def: s.f. contrac. of مکتي mukti, q.v.
s.f. A pearl (as loosened or set free from the pearl-oyster shell)
more: adj. 1. Much; enough ( kāfī ). Sauk mare to lāḍū bāṅṭūṅ, aur dān dhan dūn muḳtā, Pachpīrāṅ kī karūṅ kaḍhāī, us māṅ ghī ghālūṅ muktā . Rus. Wom. song. If but my co-wife dies I'll sweets and alms give much, For the five saints set on the pot, and butter put in much. 2. Many; a great many. Anḍe hoṅge to bachche mukte ho raheṅye . Rus. Prov. If you have the eggs you will have, plenty of chickens.

A pearl ( motī ). 1. Nak muktā bidrum bhayo op adhar kī pōë, Nain sain gunjā bhayo, haṅs muktā ho jāë . Bīhārī Satsai. Her red lips made the pearl hung from her nose red seem, Then abrus black-tipped turned beneath her black eyes' gleam, But when she laughed you saw again the pearl's soft beam. 2. Gaj muktā bānar līe, Bed līye aggyān, Param sundar yogi līe, kāyar hāth kamāṅ . Doha. To a donkey the Veds, to a monkey a pearl, To a coward a bow, to a friar a girl!
n. f. A silken dhotī , q. v.
s. m. A pearl. Also مکتا پهل मुक्ताफल muktā-phal, A pearl.
subs: s.m. A pearl; — camphor; — a custard-apple, Annona squamosa; — fine writing; a fine hand; calligraphy
or muktāvali (˚tā+āw˚), s.f. Pearl necklace; — name of various books
s.m. A string of pearls; a pearl necklace.

def: adj. (dialec., f. -ī), Much, &c. ( = muktā, q.v.). part. ety: S. मूलं
def: s.m. Root (of a plant or tree); a root (lit. and met.); basis, groundwork, beginning, principle, origin, source, cause; — bottom (of anything); foot of a mountain, &c.); lower part, or end; basis, foundation; — end, or juncture (of anything); — generation, race, stock; — original property, capital, principal, stock-in-trade (syn. aṣl); — original, original text (of any work, as distinguished from the ṭīkā, or commentary); — (in Arith.) the square root (i.q. barg-mūl; syn. jaẕr); — the twenty-fourth (or, according to some, the seventeenth, or the nineteenth) Nakshatra or lunar asterism (containing eleven stars, which appear to be the same as those in the tail of Scorpio, and are considered unlucky); — adj. & adv. Radical; original; proper; real; — radically; really
more: n. m. S. मूलम् root. See asl , 1, 3, 8, 9. 1. Stock or capital. Biyāj to chhoṛā, par mūl to de! 2. Text; the original text; context. 3. ( Arith .) The square root ( jazr ). 4. The 24th, or according to some, the 17th or 19th lunar asterism containing eleyen stars, considered unlucky. Ye laṛkā mūl meṅ janmā hai . mūl-patr , n. m. An original document. mūl gāoṅ . The original or old established village. mūl'iyā , n. m. 1. One who is born while the moon is in Scorpionis , considered unlucky. Mūliyā mā bāp pe bhārī hotā hai . 2. ( bhaḍṛī ) A soothsayer; fortune-teller. 3. (Brij) A crafty person.
s. m. 1. Origin, root, race, generation, principal or capital sum of money. 2. Text (of a book, opposed to comment). 3. Name of the nineteenth lunar mansion ( ν or υ Scorpionis). 4. adj. Really.
subs: s.m. The basis or text of a lesson, or lecture, or discourse
s.m. Original deed, document, or writing
or mūl-dhan, s.m. Original property; capital, principal, stock (syn. aṣluʼl-māl)
s.m. The essence, or drift, of wisdom
s.f. Equality with, or resemblance to, the root or origin
s.m. Connexion with the root, origin, or source
s.m. (in Gram.) A primary or simple vowel
s.m. The author of an original work; — an epithet of the Great creator
s.m. The original, or old-established, village; — the native village of the founder of a race or family
adj. (f. -vatī), Possessing roots; possessing edible roots; — practising magic with roots (?).

Domestic servants; domestics. mulāzim-i-sarkārī , n. m. G. G. A Govt. or public servant. mulāzim-i-sarkārī bannā , G. G. Personating a public servant. mulāzim k., yā banānā , v. a. To engage a servant.
(part. act. III. of لزم) adj. 1. Assiduous, diligent, attentive, attached to. 2. (subst.) A servant, attendant. (Pers. plur. ملازمان).
subs: A government servant, a public servant
To become a public servant; to personate a public servant
(-ko), To make a servant or attendant of; to engage a servant.

def: s.m. pl. of mulāzim, q.v.

ety: fr. mulāzim, q.v.
def: s.f. Attendance; service (i.q. mulāzamat).

ety: inf. n. of ملك 'to possess,' &c.
def: s.m. Dominion, sovereignty, rule, reign; a kingdom, realm, state, country, territory, region, land

s. m. A country, kingdom, realm, region, reign, sovereignty, dominion. ملک داري mulk-dār-ī, s. f. Conquest, territorial conquest, sovereignty, political affairs. ¶ malak (Heb. ) s. m. An angel. ملک الموت malaku-l-maut, The angel of death, Iﻌzrāʼīl. ¶ milk, s. f. Possession, dominion, property, right. (Plur. املاک). ¶ malik (Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. A king. (Plur. ملوک). ملک الشعرا maliku-sh-shuʼaﻌrā, Prince of poets. ملک زاده malik-zāda, s. m. A prince, a kingʼs son.
subs: s.f. Ruling, rule, sway, government, dominion, empire
s.f. Carrying on the government (of), ruling; rule, sovereignty, absolute sway

s.f. Conquest, territorial conquest, annexation; sovereignty; political affairs; — progress of a sovereign through his dominions.

ety: corr. of A. مَولَی maulā; cf. maulawī
def: s.m. A doctor, a learned man; a schoolmaster (who, in a village, has also charge of the mosque); — a judge, the deputy of a Qāzī; a jurist; — a priest; — a bigot; — (dialec.) a soothsayer, a fortune-teller; — mullā-qǒrʼānī, s.m. A law-officer who administers oaths on the Qorʼān; — one whose business it is to read the Qorʼān for forty days at the house of a deceased person
more: n. m. Cor. of مولیٰ 1. A doctor; professor. 2. A Mah. jurist; parish priest. 1. Mullā na hogā to kyā masjid meṅ azāṅ na hogī? . Prov. If there's no priest is there to be no worship? 2. Mullāṅ jī kyā kaheṅ ākhūṅ jī pahle hī samjhe hue haiṅ . Prov. What new thing can the priest say that the tutor knew not before? 3. Bed kiteb paṛheṅ, wai khutba, wai mullā, wai Pānḍe, Begar begar nām dharāë, ek māṭī ke bhānḍe, Waī khassī, wai gāë kaṭāvahīṅ, bādhī janam gaṅvāyā, Kahe Kabīr ye donoṅ bhūle, Rām kīṅhu na pāyā . Kabīr. The Mulla reads the Alcorān, the Pānde reads the Ved, By different names though them you style of one clay both are made, In slaught'ring ever goats or cows alike they waste their days, Kabīr says, 'neither e'er sees God, each from the right path strays.' 3. A judge; the deputy of a Qāzī. 4. A Mah. village schoolmaster who has also charge of the village mosque, and sometimes acts …
s. m. 1. (corrupt. of موليٰ maulạ̄ ) A schoolmaster, a doctor. 2. (corrupt. of ملواح) A decoy-bird. 3. adj. Learned (in Arabic).
subs: s.f. The office or duty of a mullā; schoolmastering, teaching, &c.

ety: fem. of māmū, q.v.; ānī = S. आनी
def: s.f. Mother's brother's wife, aunt (i.q. māmī, q.v.)
more: E. n. f. Mother's brother's wife ( māmī ); an aunt. Mūṅh par mumānī, pīṭh pīchhe gaibānī . Prov. Before my face 'Aunt,' behind my back 'thatharlot'!
s. f. An aunt, motherʼs brotherʼs wife. See مامي māmī.
subs: s.f. Husband's, or wife's, maternal aunt (i.q. mamiyā-sās).
wety: From مامُوں (māmū̃, “maternal uncle; mother's brother”) + ـَانی (-ānī).

ety: act. part., and n. of place, of مَاسَسَ, or ماسّ 'to be in contact with, to touch (another
def: part. adj. & s.m. Touching; tangent, or tangential; contiguous; — a tangent; — a place of touch or contact
more: n. f. مس Place of contact. mamās-ul-tamām ; H. sāth-chhūṭī lakīr ; S. कोटि स्यद्धिनी ( Geom .) Co-tangent. khat-i-mamās ; H. chhūtī lakīr . ( Geom .) A tangent.
(noun of place from مسّ Heb. and Chald. ) s. Place of contact, tangent.
subs: s.m. A touching line, a tangent (syn. ćhūtī lakīr).

ety: for A. مماسّة, inf. n. of ماسّ 'to touch'; see mumāss
def: s.f. Touching (another); lying with; — touch, contact; coition.
more: (inf. III. of مسّ) s. f. Touch.

ety: for A. مماطلة, inf. n. of ماطل 'to put off payment (of a debt
def: s.f. Putting off, deferring (payment of a debt, &c.); being dilatory (in performing a promise).
Mumbai (a megacity, the capital of Maharashtra, India, also known as Bombay)
wety: Borrowed from Marathi मुंबई (mumbaī).

def: Mombasa (a large coastal city in Kenya); Mombasa (a county of Kenya)
wety: Borrowed from Swahili Mombasa.

ety: act. part. of انبسط 'to be spread out; to be extended or dilated,' &c., vii of بسط 'to spread; to expand,' &c.
def: part. adj. Expanded; extended; dilated; — of an open, cheerful countenance; rejoicing, exulting, exultant.
more: (part. act. VII. of بسط) adj. Dilated, extended, rejoicing, exulting.

ety: act. part. of امدّ 'to extend, prolong; to assist,' &c.; iv of مدّ; see madd and madad
def: s.m. Extender, prolonger, protracter; — an assistant, a helper.
more: n. m. مد extending. A helper; an assistant ( sāthī ).
(part. act. IV. of مدّ) s. m. 1. An assistant. 2. An extender, protracter.

ety: prop. muʼmin, act. part. of آمن 'to render secure or safe (from
def: adj. & s.m. Believing (in God), faithful; orthodox; — a believer; an orthodox Muḥammadan (pl. mūminīn); — a Musalmān weaver.
more: n. m. 1. An orthodox Mahomedan. 2. A Mussalmān weaver.
(part. act. IV. of امن Heb. ) 1. adj. Orthodox, faithful, believing. 2. s. m. A musalmān weaver. (Plur. مومنين muminīn ).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُؤْمِن (mu'min), borrowed from Arabic مُؤْمِن (muʔmin), from آمَنَ (ʔāmana). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi مومن (mvmn /⁠momin⁠/).

ety: mūmin, q.v.+āna = Zend āna or ana = S. ana
def: adj. & adv. Faithful; orthodox (i.q. mūmin); — like a believer.
more: adj. Orthodox, faithful. adv. Like a believer.

ety: act. part. of امکن 'to give power; to be possible,' &c., iv of مکُن 'to hold rank or dignity,' &c.
def: adj. Possible, practicable, feasible; contingent; liable
more: adj. مکن being possible. Possible; practicable; feasible; can; liable; contingent. 1. Kyā mumkin jo zarā khabar tak lene āë? 2. Jiskā honā mumkin hai, uskā na honā bhī mumkin hai . What may be may not be. mumkin-ut-tabdīl ; H. palaṭne jog , adj. Commutable. mumkin-ut-taqsīm ; H. bāṅṭ-jog . adj. Partitionable; liable to partition. mumkin-ul-dakhūl , adj. Accessible. mumkin-uz-zirāٴat ; H. khetī-jog , adj Arable; culturable. mumkin-ul-vusūl, yā husūl ; H. pāne-jog . adj. Attainable. mumkin-ul-vuqū ; H. honhār , adj. 1. Possible. 2. What is to happen. mumkināt' , n. f. pl. Possibilities.
(part. act. IV. of مکن) adj. Possible. Plur. ممکنات mumkināt, Possibilities. ممکن الدخول mumkinu-d-duḵẖūl, Accessible.
subs: adj. Liable to partition; partitionable; — mumkinuʼl-ḥuṣūl, adj. Procurable; attainable
adj. Capable of cultivation, culturable; arable
adj. Possible to happen, possible; likely to arise; — what is to happen (syn. honhār).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ممکن (mumkin), from Arabic مُمْكِن (mumkin).

def: s.f. pl. (of mumkin), Possibilities; contingencies.

ety: act. part. of امسك 'to hold, hold fast, clutch,' &c., iv of مسك, idem
def: part. adj. & s.m. Holding; retaining, retentive; — economical; parsimonious, stingy, avaricious; — a miser, a niggard, a stingy fellow.
more: n. m. مسک holding fast. A miser; niggard. mum'sik , adj. 1. Retaining; retentive. 2. Stingy.
(part. act. IV. of مسک Heb. and Chald. ) 1. s. m. A miser, a niggard. 2. adj. Retaining, retentive.

ety: fr. mumsik
def: s.f. Parsimony, stinginess, avarice.

ety: pass. part. of اِمْتدّ 'to be extended,' &c. viii of مدّ 'to extend,' &c.
def: part. Extended, prolonged, protracted, stretched. ممتلی mumtalī (act. part. of اِمْتلأ 'to be filled,' &c., viii of ملأ 'to fill'), part. adj. Filled; replete; full; heaped up. ممتنع mumtaněʻ (act. part. of امتنع 'to be prohibited,' &c., viii of منع 'to prohibit,' &c.), part. adj. Prohibited, forbidden; impossible.
more: part. pass. (VIII. of مدّ Heb. and Aram. ) Extended, prolonged.

ety: act. part. of اِمتحن 'to examine, to try, prove,' &c., viii of محن 'to strike; to try, prove,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Examining, trying, proving; — examiner, tester, prover; auditor
more: n. m. محن striking. An examiner; auditor. mumtahin-i-kīmiyā , n. A chemical examiner. sarkārī mumtahin-i-kīmiyā , n. The chemical examiner to Government.
part. act. (VIII. of محن) Trying, proving, examining. ¶ mumtaḥan, part. pass. Tried, proved.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. adj. Examined, tested, tried, proved; experienced; — s.m. One who has been examined, &c.; an examinee

def: (part. pass. VIII. of منع Heb. and Chald. ) adj. Prohibited, forbidden, impossible.

ety: pass. part. of اِمتاز 'to be separated, be made distinct,' &c., viii of ماز (for
def: part. adj. Separated, or set apart (from), chosen, or selected (from); distinguished; select; exalted; illustrious, eminent, pre-eminent
more: adj. ميز distinguished. Exalted; eminent; illustrious. mumtāz k. , v. a. To exalt; elevate.
(part. pass. VIII. of ميز) adj. Distinguished; chosen, illustrious, exalted, eminent.
subs: v.t. To distinguish (a person, from, or from among, -se); to render pre-eminent, to exalt (one, above, -se)
(-meṅ), To be distinguished (in, or among); to be singled out for distinction (from among); to be pre-eminent (among); to be exalted (over, or above).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُمْتَاز (mumtāz), from Arabic مُمْتَاز (mumtāz).

def: adj. & s.m. Eager to be free (from mundane existence), striving after emancipation; — a sage abstracted from human passions, and striving after emancipation.

ety: fr. desid. of rt. मृ
def: adj. & s.m. Wishing to die; being about to die, being at the point of death; — a man at the point of death, a dying man.

s.m. = مني muni, q.v. s.m. An inspired saint, saint-sage, a holy man endowed with divine inspiration, or one who has attained more or less of a divine nature by mortification and abstraction; a pious and learned man; a sage, seer; a hermit, an ascetic, a devotee, a monk; — a term applied as a title to the Rishis and Brahmādiks, and to a great number of persons (as Agastya, Vyāsa, Pāṇini, &c.) distinguished for their writings and considered as inspired (seven of these Munis in particular are enumerated); — a symbolical expression for the number seven
subs: s.m. Dress of the Munīs (i.e. the bark of the Bhojpatr)
s.m. The duties that appertain to the status of a Muni
or (poet.) muni-rājū, or muni-rāyā, s.m. = munīśvar, q.v.
s.f. Daughter of a Muni or saint
s.m. The abode, or place of retirement of a recluse, or holy sage, &c.; a hermitage
s.m. An excellent sage, an eminent saint (i.q. muni-var, q.v.)
s.m. An epithet of respectful address to superiors
s.m. Best of ascetics or holy sages; an excellent Muni, an eminent saint; — an epithet of Śukrāćārya
s.m. An eminent ascetic or saint
vulg. munīs (˚ni+īśa), Chief of sages or ascetics; an eminent ascetic or saint
(˚ni+īś˚), s.f. Chief of sages or ascetics, &c. (i.q. munīś, q.v.); — an epithet of Vishnu, and of Buddha; — name of a commentator on the Siddhānta-Śiromaṇi
(˚ni+in˚), s.m. Chief of sages or ascetics (i.q. munīś); — an epithet of any Bauddha sage or teacher; and of Śākya-muni, and of Bharata, and of Śiva, and of a Dānava
s.m. The food of Munis or saints, &c. (consisting mostly of roots and fruits).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian من (man), ultimately from Akkadian 𒈠𒈾 (manûm).

ety: fr. mūṅdnā, q.v.
def: s.f. Shutting, closing, fastening, &c.
more: n. m. mūṅḍī, muṅḍiyā , n. f. S. मुण्ड The head. Saukan pāṛe jhūṅḍ, chhet gaū kā mūṅḍ . Rus. Her hair is by her co-wife pulled, her blows fall on the ox's head. mūṅḍ mārnā , v. a. See sir mārnā , and sir se mārnā . To fling or cast at one's head. mūṅḍ muṅḍānā , v. a. 1. See sir muṅḍānā , 2. To be shaven and shorn. 1. Mūṅḍ muṅḍāë, jaṭā rakhāë, nagan phire jūṅ bhaiṅsā, Khalṛī ūpar rākh lagāë, man jaise kā taisā . Kabīr. With shaven head and hair grown long as buffalos naked stray, Their skins all over rubbed with dust, their hearts no good betray. 2. Ghar meṅ rahe na tīrath gaë, mūṅḍ muṅḍā pha- dīhat bhaë . E. Rus. Prov. Nor stayed at home, nor pilgrimage he made, shaven and shorn with shame he was o'erlaid. 3. Je barn adham telī, kumhārā, Svapach, kirāt, kol, kalvārā, Nārī muī ghar, sampati nāsī, Mūṅḍ muṅḍāë bhaye Sanyāsī . Rāmāyan. Low castes, as oilmen, potters, and the ráce Of sweepers,…

ety: Prk. मुंडओ and मुंडअं; S. मुण्डकः and मुण्डकं

ety: mūṅg, q.v. + S. वट+इका
def: s.f. A kind of dish made with mūṅg (syn. baṛī; adaurī).
s.f. = H مونگري मूंगरी mūṅgrī, s.f. = مونگوري mūṅgaurī, q.v.
more: s. f. Name of a dish made with mūng.

def: adj. (dialec.) Headstrong, wilful, stubborn, obstinate.

ety: mūṅg, q.v.+iyā = Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: adj. Of the colour of mūṅg; green.
more: s. m. Name of a colour (red coral-coloured).

ety: from mūṅgā, q.v.; -iyā, as in the preceding
def: adj. & s.m. Coral-coloured, of the colour of red coral; — coral-colour.
s.f. = H مونگچي मूंगची mūṅgćī, s.f. = مونگوري mūṅgaurī, q.v.
more: s. f. Name of a dish made with mūng.

ety: prob. S. मङ्घूषः, rt. मङ्घ् 'to move rapidly'; cf. S. अङ्गूषः
def: s.m. (dialec.) The Mongoose, or ichneumon, Viverra ichneumon (syn. newalā or neʼolā, the more com. form).
more: s. m. (dakh.) Mongoose: see نيولا newalā.

ety: Prk. मुहं; S. मुखं
def: s.m. Mouth; face, countenance; aspect; presence; — aperture, orifice, opening; inlet, outlet; — source; — respect, regard, esteem; complaisance; partiality, bias; — strength, power; capacity, ability, fitness, qualification; pretension; pretence; — demand, charge, price (e.g. us wakīl-kā baṛā muṅh hai)
more: s. m. 1. Mouth, face, countenance, presence, aspect, orifice. 2. Respect, complaisance. 3. ( met. ) Power, fitness, qualification, pretence, ability. منہہ اترجانا muṅh utar-jānā, To have the face shrunk or withered by weakness, and emaciation. منہہ اکهري muṅh akhrī, adj. Verbal. منہامنہہ muṇh-ā-muṅh, adj. Brimful. منہہ آنا muṅh ānā, Salivation. منہہ اندهيرا muṅh andherā, 1. Twilight. 2. Evening. 3. Dusk, obscure. منہہ بارنا muṅh bārnā, To refrain. منہہ بانا muṅh bānā, To open the mouth, to gape. منہہ بگاڙنا muṅh bigāṛnā, To frown, to make faces. منہہ بگڙنا muṅh bigaṛnā, 1. To be displeased. 2. To have the taste blunted or depraved. منہہ بنانا muṅh banānā, To make mouths or faces, to fashion the face. منہہ بند کرنا muṅh band karnā, To hold oneʼs tongue. منہہ بولا muṅh-bolā, adj. Nominal, in name or appellation. منہہ بولا بهاٴي muṅh-bolā bhāʼī, or منہہ بولي بہن muṅh-bolī-bahan, An intimate…

or muṅh uṭhāʼe, adv. With the face up, or turned upwards; without looking before one; blindly; head-foremost, headlong
adv. On waking, or rising (from bed)
v.n. 'The face to be bright or white,' not to incur disgrace or dishonour; to come off with credit, or with flying colours
adj. & adv. By word of mouth, verbal; — verbally (syn. zabānī)
or muṅhāṅ-muṅh, adj. Face to face; to one's face; — brimful (syn. lab-ā-lab); — muṅhā-muṅh bharnā, v.t. To fill to the brim; to stuff; to overstock
(-ko), To censure (one) to his face; — to slap (one) on the face
(-kā), To be or become salivated; — to have the thrush; — to utter taunts (against, -par); — s.m. Salivation
adj. (f. -ī), Dark, obscure; — s.m. Dusk, twilight; evening; dawn of day, daybreak; — muṅh-andhere, adv. Before daybreak; at dawn
To open the mouth, to gape; — to give a forced laugh; — to expire
'To sit with the mouth bound up or closed,' to sit silent
To make a wry face, to look displeased or sour, to frown or scowl

(apnā, or kisī-kā), To make a wry face, to frown, scowl; to look sulky, to pout; — to make faces; — to spoil or disfigure the face (of another); to put one out of countenance; — to leave a bad taste in the mouth
v.n. 'The face to be soured,' to be displeased; — to have the taste blunted or depraved; — to have a bad taste in the mouth
To fashion the face (of, -kā); — to make faces (at), to mock; — to express feeling through the features
s.m. A muzzle; — adj. With the mouth closed
To hold one's tongue; — to stop the mouth (of, -kā), to silence; to give hush-money (to), to bribe (i.q. muṅh bharnā)
s.m. pl. A sect of Sewṛās who keep a cloth hanging before their mouths
To make (oneself), or to put on, a good face; — to fit or qualify oneself (for)
adj. (f. -bolī), Nominal, in name or appellation, so-called; adopted
s.m., or muṇh-bolī bahan, s.f. A brother, or a sister, so-called, an adopted brother or sister; an intimate friend
v.n. The month to water; — to feel nausea, to be squeamish
s.f. Hush-money, a bribe, a sop
adv. 'Filling the mouth'; fully, completely, thoroughly (used by women; cf. peṭ-bhar-ke)
(-kā), To fill the mouth (of); to stop the mouth (of) with a sop, to give hush-money (to); to bribe
(-kā), To find (one) favourably disposed; to get into the good graces (of); to presume on the favour (of)
adv. On or upon (one's) mouth or face; — before the face, in the presence (of, -ke, or mere, &c.)
(-ke), To spit on the face (of); — (fig̠.) to disgrace, or insult
(-ke), To be restrained through respect or regard (for another)
'Not to put a grain to the mouth,' to go without a morsel of food
'A dove to fly across the face (of),' to change colour; to be or become confounded; to look blank or astounded
(-ke), To get warm or angry in the presence (of); to behave disrespectfully in the presence (of a superior)
To bring to the lips; to utter, say, speak of, tell, relate
To place a seal on the lips (of, -apne, or uske, &c.), to seal the lips
'To be without a nose on the face'; to be shameless
(or hawāʼiyāṅ) uṛnā, or phirnā, or phir-jānā, or ćhuṭnā, 'To have a squib or rocket discharged against one's face'; to be or become confounded; to change colour; to look blank or astonished
(kisī-ke), To fall into the mouth (of); — to be eaten; — to behave with boldness or presumption (before)
adj. f. (m. -paṛā), On the tongues (of people; — syn. zabān-zad)
v.n. To open the mouth wide, to gape (with wonder, &c.)
adj. & s.m. Outspoken, fearless, unconcerned; foul-mouthed, abusive; — an outspoken, or fearless, &c. person; — a vain babbler
v.n. The face to be turned or distorted, the face to be paralyzed; — the face to be turned away (from, -se), to be satiated or cloyed; to be disgusted (with); to be displeased (with)
To distend the cheeks; to make a wry face, &c. (i.q. muṅh bigāṛnā, q.v.)
'To burst open the lips and speak'; to break silence; to make bold to say
(-se, or -kī t̤araf-se), To turn the face away (from); to be averse (to); — to abstain (from); to avoid, shun
To gape (i.q. muṅh bānā, and muṅh pasārnā), to yawn; — to desire much; — to presume; — to demand a high price (for); to raise one's price
(-kā), To gaze, or stare, at the face (of); to be astonished, or afflicted; to look blank, or foolish
Just look at his face! what assurance! what cheek! — muṅh toṛnā (-kā), To break the mouth (of); — to harass
muṅh thutānā or thuthānā = muṅh banānā
(-kā), To slap the face (of), to box the ears (of)
To distort the face; to make wry faces (see muṅh bigāṛnā)
'To join face (to face),' to draw close together and whisper; to talk scandal in an under-tone
(-kā), To lick the face (of); to caress; to make much (of), to curry favour (with), to toady
(-kā), To make mouths or faces (at), to mock; — to make a bad imitation (of), to caricature
adj. Attached (to), intimate (with); possessing the ear (of), having an ascendancy (over)
(-ke), To be or become familiar or intimate (with); to attach oneself (to); — to face, confront (with hostile intention)
To bite, to snap (as a horse); to open the mouth (to devour); — to work the mouth or jaws, to eat
The mouth to be moving or working; to chew the cud; — to be foul-mouthed
adj. Sheepish, shamefaced, bashful, timid; — keeping at home, not visiting friends
(-se), To hide or veil the face (from bashfulness, &c.); to keep parda; — to be bashful; — to be in hiding, to hide, skulk; — to avoid, shun, to evade
s.m. & f. One who hides his (or her) face; — a bashful person; — a skulker, &c.; — blind-man's buff
(-kā), To touch the face (of); — to give (one) a formal invitation (to)
adv. Face to face, before, in the presence (of)
(-ko), To show one's face (to), to appear before (one) with confidence and satisfaction (e.g. kyā muṅh dikhāʼūṅgā? 'how can I show my face?')
s.f. A present given (by the female relations of the bridegroom, and the friends of the family) on the first visit to a bride
(-ke, &c.), To continue staring at (one) with surprise, to be lost in astonishment
To say pleasant things (to), to flatter

(apnā, or kisī-kā, &c.), To see one's face (in a glass); — to look at the face (of); to look about idly; — to stare at in astonishment or helplessness; — to look to (one, for aid, &c.), to depend (on); — to have a regard (for one)
or prīt, or ćāh, or maḥabbat, Show of regard or friendship before a person; apparent friendship or affection
muṅh-dekhī bāt kahnā = muṅh dekh-kar bāt kahnā
(-meṅ), To put the mouth or lips (into, or to); — to make familiarized, to favour, to countenance
(-meṅ), To put the mouth (into, or to); to thrust the muzzle (into); to eat; — to bite (a horse); to peck or seize with the beak (a cock, in fighting); — to beg, request
To keep a good countenance; to keep on good terms (with)
adj. Verbal (message, or evidence, &c.), oral, vivâvoce
'The face to become yellow,' to turn pale (from fear)
Hard-mouthed (a horse); restive; — headstrong, stubborn, obstinate; — outspoken, &c. (i.q. muṅh-phaṭ, q.v.)
s.f. Hardness of mouth; headstrongness, &c.; — foul-mouthedness, abusiveness
To puff out the cheeks; to pout, to sulk; — to cause the face (of another, -kā, &c.) to swell (by a slap, or blow; — i.q. muṅh phulānā, and muṅh bigāṛnā)
To contract the face, to make a wry face
To bridle the mouth or tongue; to hold one's tongue
The mouth to become dry or parched; — the face to become thin or emaciated
(-ke, or mere, &c.), To take the words out of the mouth (of); to anticipate (and to prevent from speaking) one who is on the point of speaking
Flowers (of rhetoric) to fall from the lips (of, -ke); — (ironic.) to rate in round terms, to reproach, to abuse
Milk to drop from the mouth (of); to be yet a child, to have no sense
(-kā), To sew up the mouth (of); — to close or seal the lips (of, i.q. muṅh-par mǒhr karnā); to silence (a person); — to give hush-money (to, i.q. muṅh bharnā)
(-kā), To change colour, to turn pale (esp. from fear)
To blacken one's own face; to incur disgrace; — to blacken the face (of another, -kā); to disgrace, bring disgrace on; to punish; — to turn out with disgrace; to break off all connection (with)
The face to be or become black or blackened; to incur disgrace, be disgraced
muṅh-kā laććhan jhaṛnā = muṅh-kī loʼī utarnā
'With the mouth's (morsel) in the mouth and the hand's (spoonful) in the hand'; staggered, aghast
s.m. A morsel; — anything easily attained

(-kī t̤araf, or kī or), To turn the face (to or towards), to direct the steps (towards); to face, confront; — to attack; to abuse; — to regard with favour, be partial (to); — to direct the attention (to), to be greedy or miserly; to come to a head, to burst or, open (as a boil, or an abscess, &c.); — to make the first attempt at seizing game (a young hound, or other animal used in hunting)
(-ke), To get a taste for blood; vice to grow (on one)
The mouth to become open; — to become foul-mouthed or abusive
(-kā), To cause (one's) mouth to become opened; to make (or to provoke one to) speak
To open the mouth; — to abuse; — to uncover the face; to present to view; to reveal
(or bhal) girnā, To fall on the face, or face foremost
muṅh-ke kawwe uṛ-jānā = muṅh-par fāḵẖta uṛ-jānā
To eat one's own words; to be convicted out of one's own mouth; — to be browbeaten; — to receive a blow on the face; — to suffer disgrace; to be put to the blush
(-ke), To plug the mouth (of), to gag; to silence; — to tie the tongue (of a person) with a charm or spell; — to exorcise
or jānā, To lose (one's) brightness of countenance, or (one's) good looks; — to lose the sense of shame; — to lose (one's) good manners, to become corrupted, or depraved
To redden the mouth or lips; — to make the face (of another, -kā) red (by slapping)
v.n. The mouth or lips to be tinged with red; — the face to become red, or to flush (with anger, &c.); — to be honoured, to get a good name
'To muffle up the face and lie down'; to lie down in the sulks; to take to one's bed in grief
To hang down the face or head; — to make a long face, to be down in the mouth
To apply the mouth or lips (to, -meṅ)

(-ke, or mere, &c.), To have the mouth burned or affected (by a pungent substance, &c.); — to suit the mouth or palate (of), to be agreeable to the taste (of); — to become used, or accustomed (to), or familiarized (with); — to become intimate (with), to become a favourite (of); — to dispute, or bandy words (with)
'To attack (one) with the mouth,' to bite; to sting; to say biting or stinging things; — to attack, to fall upon; — to stop the mouth (of a person, -kā); to silence; — to feed
or muṅh-māgā, adj. (f. -ī), Demanded, requested, or asked for by word of mouth
s.m. Price demanded or asked for; — muṅh-māṅgī murād, s.f. Any thing or object obtained in accordance with expressed wish; — heart-felt wish
s.f. The death asked or wished for
To make a wry face; — to turn up the nose; — to frown, to be displeased, or offended

(-se), To turn away the face (from); to disregard; to be averse (to); to avoid, shun; to abstain or desist (from); — to turn (against), to rebel, revolt; — to turn a deaf ear (to); to give the cold shoulder (to)
(-kā), To sweeten the mouth (of); to give (one) a treat; — to give a sop or bribe (to)
The mouth to water; to feel eager desire (for); — to be tempted, or seduced
(-ke), To drop water into the mouth (of); — to perform the last offices (for)
To read to oneself, to mumble
To take a straw in one's mouth (as a sign of humility, or submission); to humble or abase oneself, to lick the dust; — to submit (to), resign oneself (to)
s.m.f.(?) The mouth-piece of a ḥuqqa
To look blank or foolish; — to simper
v.n. Not to have the face (to)
v.n. The cheeks to sink, &c. (i.q. muṅh utar-jānā, q.v.)
'The face and hands to break'; the limbs to be broken; to meet with a bad accident
To wash the face and hands
To return (from any enterprise) with a face (expressive of disappointment) peculiarly one's own; to return disappointed; to fail of success
To put the hand to the mouth (in admiration, or astonishment, or shame) and remain motionless; to be astonished, or ashamed, &c.
or ḵẖāk tere muṅh-meṅ, Dirt in your mouth! may your mouth be fouled!
(May his, or your,) face (become) black! may disgrace overtake him!

Lālā, gon kā moṅḍhā karo! Sir, open your sack! 4. Makān kā muṅh baṛā sakṛā hai . 4. Presence ( huzūrī , 1). Muṅh se mahābā . Prov. The master's presence keeps the servants in awe. 5. Regard; esteem ( lihāz ). 1. Kutte! terā …

def: s.m. corr. of A. munīb, q.v. the first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets. Its pronunciation depends on the vowel which accompanies it; e.g. ا with fatḥa (اَ) = the Devanāgarī अ = the English a, as in cedar (or = the English u in fun); ا with kasra (اِ) = the Devanāgarī इ = the English i, as in fin; ا with ẓamma (اُ) = the Devanāgarī उ = the English u, as in bull. — ا with fatḥa, followed by ا, (i.e. اَا, which is usually written آ) = the Devanāgarī आ = the English ā, as in father; ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اَو) = the Devanāgarī औ = the English au (sounded like ou in house); ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter ي, (i.e. اَي), = the Devanāgarī ऐ = the English ai, as in aisle. — ا with ẓamma, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اُو), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ऊ = the English u, as in rule (or = the oo in pool), and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ओ = the English o, as in pole. — ا with kasra, followed by ي, (i.e. اِي), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ई = the English ī, as in caprice; and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ए = the French e, as in fête. ا is used, — 1˚ (arithmetically, according to the Abjad, q.v.) for the number one. 2˚ (for अ, in Almanacks and Astrology) for Sunday, and Aries. 3˚ (for अ) as a name of the god Vishnu. 4˚ as an inseparable prefix to a word beginning with a consonant, to signify negation or privation: e.g. aśuddha, 'impure,' 'incorrect.' As in Greek, a becomes an before a vowel; e.g. ananta, 'without end,' 'endless.'
more: n. m. Cor. of A. munīb . 1. A Hindī accountant or clerk. 2. ( munīm dīvān ) An agent; a manager. munīm'ī , n. f. The office of a munīm .

(˚ni+īś˚), s.f. Chief of sages or ascetics, &c. (i.q. munīś, q.v.); — an epithet of Vishnu, and of Buddha; — name of a commentator on the Siddhānta-Śiromaṇi
(˚ni+in˚), s.m. Chief of sages or ascetics (i.q. munīś); — an epithet of any Bauddha sage or teacher; and of Śākya-muni, and of Bharata, and of Śiva, and of a Dānava
s.m. The food of Munis or saints, &c. (consisting mostly of roots and fruits).

def: s.f. The female of the little birds called amadavats (or (lāl), Fringilla amandava
more: n. f. The female amadavat. munyā bolnā , v. n. To have a good omen; forebode good.
s. f. The female of the little birds called amadavats (or lal. Fringilla amandava).
subs: 'The Muniyā to pipe' (regarded as a good omen); to have a good omen in the piping of the muniyā.

ety: Prk. मुंजो; S. मुञ्जः; see munj
def: s.f. A sort of rush or grass (of which ropes are made), Saccharum munja
more: n. f. S. मुञ्ज Pālī, munjo . A kind of rush or grass. It grows on sandy soil in the rains, and is cut for making cord from its fibre ( saccharum munjā ). Nāle mūṅj bagar, nāle debī dā darshan . Panj. Prov. Both mūnj and bagar , and also the worship of the goddess! (The neighbourhood of the temple of Chintpurne abounds in mūnj and bagar , the worshippers returning with loads of these grasses of which ropes are made). mūṅj kā bān , Rope made of mūṅj .
s. f. The name of a grass of which ropes are made. (Saccharum munja: Roxb. ).

def: s.m. (dialec.) = منجا munjā, q.v. s.m. A sort of rush or grass, Saccharum munja (from the fibres of which a string is prepared, of which the Brāhmaṇical girdle is, or should be, formed; it is also used in basket-work, &c.); the Brāhmaṇical girdle, or sacred cord of a Brāhmaṇ; — an arrow.

ety: prob. S. मुंजकः or मौञ्जकः
def: s.m. A thread-like worm, produced in the eye of a horse.

s.m. A sort of rush or grass, Saccharum munja (from the fibres of which a string is prepared, of which the Brāhmaṇical girdle is, or should be, formed; it is also used in basket-work, &c.); the Brāhmaṇical girdle, or sacred cord of a Brāhmaṇ; — an arrow.
s.m. The kernel of the nut of the tāṛ or Borassus flabelliformis (especially when it is soft and edible).
